mnemonics.title: Constructors and Destructors
mnemonics.content: Constructors are called when an object is created, and destructors are called when an object is about to be destroyed.

acronyms.usage: A constructor is a special member function of a class that is used to initialize objects.
A destructor is a special member function of a class that is used to clean up objects.

visualTips.visualization: Think of constructors and destructors as the setup and cleanup crew for objects. Constructors set up the object and initialize its data members, while destructors clean up the object and release any resources it has acquired.

analogies.analogy: A constructor is like a setup crew that prepares a stage for a performance. Just as the setup crew sets up the stage and prepares it for the performance, a constructor sets up an object and initializes its data members.

A destructor is like a cleanup crew that cleans up the stage after a performance. Just as the cleanup crew cleans up the stage and removes any trash or debris, a destructor cleans up an object and releases any resources it has acquired.
